frameworks.name: games as experimental paradigm
frameworks.one_line: Games used as ecologically rich platforms to probe cognition.

frameworks.name: inductive bias
frameworks.one_line: Intuitiveness of games reveals biases supporting behavior in complex settings.

frameworks.name: intrinsic motivation
frameworks.one_line: Fun and play motivate study of new cognitive questions.

core_question: How can games serve as an experimental platform to study the mind beyond traditional lab paradigms?
key_assumptions: Games are intuitive and tap natural inductive biases
Fun broadens participation and data scale
Games approximate ecological complexity better than lab tasks
key_findings: Games offer a vantage point on inductive biases supporting ecological behavior
Games enable study of play and intrinsic motivation
Games attract larger and more diverse participant pools
interpretations: Games can elevate ecological validity, scale, and robustness of cognition research
Wider adoption of games as paradigms is recommended
limitations_noted: Drawbacks of games relative to standard lab experiments are acknowledged
